brand.name: Actalent Services
brand.vertical: B2B/professional — engineering & sciences staffing and technical services

summary: Actalent Services provides engineering and sciences staffing (contract, contract-to-hire, and direct placement) plus project-based technical services such as engineering services, clinical development, and quality/project management consulting. It serves engineering and R&D leaders, project management offices, and talent acquisition teams at mid-to-large enterprises in regulated industries — life sciences, aerospace, utilities, transportation, and semiconductor — who need specialized technical talent faster than internal recruiting can deliver. Deals are enterprise B2B, sold through account managers, with no self-serve path.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding (Employer Inquiry)
campaigns.objective: Activate a new employer inquiry by setting expectations, introducing the dedicated account manager, and driving toward a booked consultation call — the first-value moment.
campaigns.trigger: Contact form submission with inquiry type = Talent Solutions / Engineering / Sciences / Specialized (employer side); send within seconds.
campaigns.segment: New employer contacts; branch by primary-need category and timeline urgency (Immediate Need <1 month vs. Seeking Consultation).
campaigns.rationale: Actalent's post-inquiry follow-up is handled manually by account managers, leaving a window where high-intent buyers cool off. An instant, human-voiced welcome that names a real account manager captures urgency and routes the buyer straight to a consultation.

emails.purpose: Confirm the inquiry was received, set expectations on what happens next, introduce the dedicated account manager by name, and deliver the first win — a booked consultation slot.
emails.subject: Your engineering talent request is in — here's your next step
emails.preview: A dedicated Actalent partner is already reviewing your need.
keyPoints: Confirm receipt and reassure on speed — qualified candidate submittals typically begin within 48–72 hours of a defined requisition
Introduce a named account manager as a single point of contact, with a real reply-to address
Reference their stated need and timeline urgency to make it feel personally routed
One clear CTA to book the consultation; reply-to-this-email option for immediate needs

campaigns.name: Consultation-to-Contract Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert employer prospects who completed a free consultation but haven't yet signed an SOW or MSA by handling the cost objection and reinforcing talent-pool quality.
campaigns.trigger: Consultation call completed; no signed agreement after a defined follow-up window.
campaigns.segment: Post-consult employer prospects; branch by industry (life sciences, aerospace, utilities, semiconductor) and by whether a proposal/SOW has been issued.
campaigns.rationale: This is Actalent's core revenue play — the deal is closed by a human, but a structured nurture keeps the proposal alive between touchpoints. The #1 objection is staffing markup vs. direct/offshore hire, so the hero send must reframe cost as risk-and-schedule protection with proof.

emails.purpose: Recap the consultation, directly address the markup-vs-direct-hire cost objection by quantifying the cost of schedule slip and mis-hires, and back it with a relevant industry case study.
emails.subject: What a 90-day skills gap really costs your project
emails.preview: Why the right specialist beats the cheaper headcount.
keyPoints: Reframe markup as protection against schedule risk, compliance exposure, and the cost of a mis-hire in a niche discipline
Lead with a peer case study from their industry showing on-time delivery and protected margin
Reinforce talent-pool depth and vetting quality for their specific role type
Single CTA to review the proposal/SOW; offer a call with the account manager to walk through it

campaigns.name: Win-Back — Dormant Clients & Candidates
campaigns.objective: Reactivate client accounts with no requisition in 90+ days and re-warm dormant candidate profiles before they join a competitor's roster.
campaigns.trigger: Client inactivity threshold (90+ days, no new requisition) or contractor between placements; cancellation/account-reset events.
campaigns.segment: Dormant employer accounts (often post-project-wrap or new procurement contact) handled separately from dormant candidates, who get a placement-focused message.
campaigns.rationale: Lapsed clients and a cold candidate pool are Actalent's biggest churn risks, and reactivation is fast to ship and protects deliverability. A market-intelligence hook re-opens the conversation without discounting — leading with value, which fits the B2B trust model.

emails.purpose: Re-open the relationship with dormant client accounts using a fresh labor-market hook tied to their discipline, and offer a frictionless way to restart a requisition.
emails.subject: The engineering talent market shifted — here's what changed in your field
emails.preview: A quick read for the next time a project resource gap hits.
emails.cta: See this quarter's labor market report
keyPoints: Lead with value — current hiring-difficulty and time-to-fill data for their specialty, not a sales ask
Acknowledge it's been a while and reintroduce their account manager / note any new point of contact
Remind them of the core value: specialized talent ready in days when the next project gap hits
Low-friction CTA — read the report, with a one-line option to reply and reopen a requisition

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture (Market Reports & Whitepapers)
campaigns.objective: Move cold leads who downloaded a market report or whitepaper toward sales-readiness across a long cycle, then route high-intent leads to the consultation play.
campaigns.trigger: Gated-content download (Labor Market Intelligence report, clinical-trial or infrastructure-risk whitepaper, case study).
campaigns.segment: Leads below the sales-ready lead-score threshold; branch by the topic of the asset downloaded and by industry/role where known.
campaigns.rationale: Actalent already produces strong lead magnets but has no evidence of a nurture series following download, so high-intent content downloaders go untouched. A buyer-journey-mapped sequence with lead scoring focuses account managers on the leads most likely to convert.

emails.purpose: Deliver the requested asset instantly and frame the real business problem behind it — the project and compliance risk of unfilled specialized roles.
emails.subject: Your labor market report is inside — plus the gap most teams miss
emails.preview: The data behind your hardest-to-fill engineering roles.
keyPoints: Deliver the downloaded asset immediately with a clean link, no friction
Frame the underlying problem the data exposes — rising time-to-fill and compliance risk in regulated disciplines
Set expectations for a short series of useful, no-fluff follow-ups over the coming weeks
Soft secondary path to a consultation for readers with an immediate need

campaigns.name: Renewal & Account Expansion
campaigns.objective: Protect relationship-recurring revenue ahead of placement end dates and MSA reviews, and grow accounts into adjacent service lines and added headcount.
campaigns.trigger: Placement end date or contract renewal − 60/90 days; annual MSA review; usage milestone signaling expansion readiness.
campaigns.segment: Active client accounts approaching a renewal or placement end; branch by account health (high engagement = expansion offer, low = save/re-onboard).
campaigns.rationale: Actalent's revenue is structurally recurring through rolling contracts and renewing MSAs, and contract end-dates are a high-value, automatable gap. A value-recap send ahead of renewal quantifies the outcome delivered and opens the door to extension and adjacent service lines before a competitor does.

emails.purpose: Quantify the value delivered this period — placements made, roles filled, schedule risk avoided — and tee up extension plus adjacent service-line expansion.
emails.subject: Your year with Actalent, by the numbers
emails.preview: Placements delivered, gaps closed, and what's next for your team.
keyPoints: Lead with the account's real outcomes — contractors placed, niche roles filled, time-to-fill achieved
Connect those wins to upcoming project needs and a smooth contract extension path
Surface one adjacent service line (e.g., engineering services or sciences staffing) as a natural expansion
CTA to schedule a planning review with the account manager ahead of the renewal date
